Alright, welcome back. We're really at the good stuff now in terms of how you truly create a custom for life and how you monetize that. Here's the deal. Now, once you close a sale, all the things I've been talking about up to this point were about making sure that you kept the relationship not just intact but building on itself and building on itself to the point where by the time the person is finished onboarding, and that could be within a day or two or longer in a longer sale cycle situation where you have a more complex product and this integration is required, right? But when you're done with the onboarding phase of your product, right, and it's now rolled out into adoption, well, guess what? You're going to have massive opportunities here to do upsells and cross-sells and also get referrals. So, let me handle those one at a time, explain what they are first and then how you go about maximizing the opportunities while maintaining the ethical underpinnings that you're only going to sell things to people that need them, want them, and can benefit from them, right? And also without seeming salesy and pushing. In other words, it happens almost organically, so to speak, all right? Let's start with nomenclature first. An upsell refers to when you sell someone more or more features of the same product. In other words, let's say you sell them a certain program and it's an online program and the program allows people to have access to X number of videos and so forth, right? An upsell would be where they get the same product, but now they can actually have it in high definition or some interactive opportunity or access to the archives of that particular product. In other words, it's same product is being sold, but more features of that product and greater access to it, so to speak. That's an upsell as opposed to what's called a cross-sell, which means you're now selling an entirely different product. You get it? So, a cross-sell would be I sold you product A, let's say in the case of straight line, right? It's an online training for the straight line. Well, a cross-sell would be if I now said, hey, listen, I know you also need to get leads. My next plan is getting on. You want to learn how to get leads? I have straight line lead generation, an entire different product that I think would really help you out. If you have 60 seconds, I'd like to tell you a bit more about it. You got a minute? And now if they're really happy, and that's not the pitch, so I'm just going to, I can't help myself, right? But the point is that's a cross-sell. So an upsell would be if I sold you more features of this product, a cross-sell would be if I sold you an entirely different product. You get it? Why is that important? Well, it's just important to have the right terminology, as we know we're talking about as we go forward. And then lastly, we also have that third element, which is referrals, which I'm going to handle in a completely separate lesson here. We're going to actually show you a five-step process of how you generate a massive sumo referrals that goes on and on. And that's just the best because referrals are the easiest closes of all. Why? Well, because you already have those second and third tens pretty much established by a reference, meaning that someone has called their friends and say, oh my God, this is the greatest guy, and the company's awesome. They give great customer service. And maybe someone, the first tens, or the product trade, but really they've sold you. That's what a referral really does. Some about the product, so the three tens are really more established, especially that second one, so they easy it to close. Make sense? Okay, great. So let's now start with upsells. Here's the deal. Because there are so many of you and going through this training and so many different industries offline, online, I can't give you one specific formula that goes for every single industry and every single product within it. So let me give you more of the guidelines of how you go about doing this. And then in the exercises in the back end, I'll take you through how you go about taking that and essentially chunking it down so it fits your industry perfectly. You get it. You adapt it to your industry the way we did with our patterns for looping. So let's talk about a general understanding of how you upsell. Essentially you sell someone a product, they buy it, they close, they pay. That's very important to make sure they pay because what happens is sometimes with inexperienced salesperson, they make a sale. Perhaps there's a collection going on here where it might take two weeks for the person to pay or there's a long term payment plan and they start trying to upsell other stuff and the person really hasn't committed it. You get me now having paid and you blow the whole sale up. For example, in the brokerage industry, the way it works is based on what product you're selling the customer will have between one and five or seven days to pay depending on the product. Well, what you wouldn't do is close the person on a Monday, they haven't paid till Wednesday, a Thursday, right? And on Tuesday, you try to sell them more stuff. Not going to work out very well. Now when would the best time to sell that person product be? Well, it depends. Let me just give you a broad formula that goes for all businesses. Here's what it is. It starts with the understanding that there's no written law. Nothing set in stone that says you cannot upsell a customer or for that matter, cross sell to a customer or for that matter, ask for referral from a customer until they've experienced all the wonderful features and benefits and have had their pain resolved by the purchase they made. This is the biggest mistake, the biggest misconception that young salespeople have. And I said young, I mean, young in the industry, they could be all but just new sales with newbies, right? In other words, I sell someone a product and I close them right now. When would be the appropriate time to ask for an upsell or cross sell? Well, upsells and cross sells, it would really be the same process for both. The question would be, what is in the best interest of the client? In other words, maybe it makes sense for the person to upsell. There are certain features that the person didn't take on the first go on. You did a step down sale. So you said, you know, let's get started. We'll start with the basic package here. It's a question of only a cash outlay of 499, blah, whatever it might be, blah, blah, blah, right? And you left out five or six features of this product or something that would allow them to get more benefits. And because you left it out to get the account open, what would happen is the person pays, they check it out, they go, wow, this is really good. And literally the day the check hit, I'd be calling that person back. The day the check or the wire hit, okay, I'd be calling that person back and trying to upsell or cross sell them if it was in their best interest. However, that being said, what I would also do is before I made that sales call, I would have at least one call in between and also an outgoing email. I would have at least two touch points before I actually went to close. So watch, let's just do this realistically. Today is Monday. I closed the sale today. Okay. And let's just say that the wire hit, you could actually, you know, it was the same day you critical cleared, right? Done, right? No free trials, no 30 day, well, 30 day money back there, no free trial. So it's locked in, right? Would I upsell you tomorrow? No, I wouldn't. That's too soon. What I would do is I would first make sure that you got the prop that you were onboarded correctly. In other words, when I'd say, Hey, you're John, you're doing just calling you back the next day, just checking in, make sure everything's going well. Now again, there's so many difference there. So I don't know whether you're doing the onboarding, you have a customer success department, it could be self onboarding with an intuitive product, whatever the case is, I as the salesperson who is looking to upsell this prospect, this client, I am going to make sure that I speak to him once, check in, ask him how things are going and have a general call schmooze calls. I mentioned in the last lesson, when we bond, I gather any more intelligence that was missing, identify his aliveness and so forth. That's what I'm going to do. I'm not going to try to sell something before I do that. In addition, I'm going to have sent out that thank you note, either a hand written thank you note, or at least an email or a text to let them know that I really do care about them, that I'm appreciative that gave me their business and that I'm looking forward to a long term relationship. I want to give them my personal information with my phone number, my cell number, right? My email is going to make sure that I let them understand I'm accessible on the case, I'm there to support them, but I'm not looking to sell them anything. That's my first call after I close. And along with that would be a touch point by either email, text and along with text to be a WhatsApp or any other ways you keep in touch with online mechanisms. Right? Now, let's say that happened on Wednesday. So I let a day rest two days later. I called no sale made schmooze call. I update them what's going on and say how things going and again, whatever it is, you know, I, if it's only different products, right? You know, everything's looking great. I hope you're enjoying it or any questions that might have any issues that they're not clear on. I resolved them and that's it. We talk about maybe some personal stuff for about 30 seconds to a minute. I'm not going to go on and on, but if they want to talk at this point, I will let them, I'll actually ask probing questions. In other words, this whole idea, as I mentioned in the last lesson, going off to Pluto, different definition now that they're actually your client. See a real expert, once they close someone with in reason, they want to know about their client person, what makes them tick, you know, what makes them happy life, what makes them sick, what their problems, what the challenges are so they can help them as many areas as possible. They want to know about their family life, how many children have what the birthdays are out of versus if it's appropriate. Again, you have to be smart about this, but you want to have that information on hand for the long term. So now birthday cause, gifts. I'll discuss ethical gifts in the next lesson as well. Okay.
